results.title: Paper page - Qwen-Image-Agent: Bridging the Context Gap in Real-World Image Generation
results.content:
While text-to-image (T2I) models have achieved remarkable progress, they struggle with real-world requests that are often underspecified, implicit, or dependent on up-to-date knowledge. We identify this challenge as the Context Gap: the mismatch between the user context and the sufficient generation context for T2I models. To bridge this gap, we propose Qwen-Image-Agent, a unified agentic framework that integrates plan, reason, search, memory and feedback in a context-centric manner. Qwen-Image-Agent treats user input as partial context and progressively constructs the generation context through Context-Aware Planning and Context Grounding. Specifically, Context-Aware Planning identifies missing context and plans how it should be acquired and used, while Context Grounding gathers this [...] Hugging Face's logo

# Qwen-Image-Agent: Bridging the Context Gap in Real-World Image Generation

Qwen

## Abstract

A unified agentic framework called Qwen-Image-Agent is proposed to address the context gap in text-to-image generation by progressively constructing complete generation context through planning, reasoning, searching, and memory mechanisms. [...] be acquired and used, while Context Grounding gathers this context from reason, search, memory, and feedback. To evaluate agentic image generation, we further introduce Image Agent Bench (IA-Bench), a benchmark covering four core image agent capabilities: Plan, Reason, Search, and Memory. Experiments on IA-Bench, Mindbench and WISE-Verified show that Qwen-Image-Agent outperforms strong baselines and achieves state-of-the-art performance.

results.title: Bridging the Context Gap in Real-World Image Generation | alphaXiv
results.content:
### Summary

Qwen-Image-Agent is introduced as an end-to-end framework from the Qwen team, designed to bridge the "Context Gap" in real-world image generation by integrating planning, reasoning, search, memory, and feedback. The framework establishes state-of-the-art performance on the newly proposed IA-Bench benchmark with an IA-score of 45.4%, and also achieves top results on WISE-Verified (0.9020) and MindBench (0.42), demonstrating its ability to handle underspecified and knowledge-dependent requests. [...] Qwen-Image-Agent is introduced as an end-to-end framework from the Qwen team, designed to bridge the "Context Gap" in real-world image generation by integrating planning, reasoning, search, memory, and feedback. The framework establishes state-of-the-art performance on the newly proposed IA-Bench benchmark with an IA-score of 45.4%, and also achieves top results on WISE-Verified (0.9020) and MindBench (0.42), demonstrating its ability to handle underspecified and knowledge-dependent requests.

## Table of Contents

### The Problem of the Context Gap in Image Generation

### A Unified Framework for Agentic Generation

### Context-Aware Planning

### Grounding the Generation Context

### Grounding via Reason

### Grounding via Search

### Grounding via Memory

### Grounding via Feedback [...] The researchers identify this discrepancy as the "Context Gap." Formally, this is the mismatch between the user-provided context cu​ (which may be a short, vague, or incomplete prompt) and the comprehensive generation context cg​ required by a T2I model to produce a satisfactory result. While a human designer can infer that a request for "the current stock price of Apple" requires a web search and a specific layout, a standard T2I model typically lacks the agentic capabilities to bridge this gap on its own.

# [Literature Review] Qwen-Image-Agent: Bridging the Context Gap in Real-World Image Generation

The paper introduces Qwen-Image-Agent, a unified, context-centric agentic framework designed to bridge the "Context Gap"—the structural mismatch between sparse user-provided prompts and the detailed, comprehensive context required for successful real-world image generation.

### Core Methodology [...] ### Core Methodology

The framework treats image generation not as a direct rendering task, but as a multi-step construction process where the agent progressively builds a complete generation context ($c\_g$) from an underspecified user context ($c\_u$). The process is formulated as:
$$p\_{\text{agent}}(y | c\_u) = \sum\_{\tau} p(\tau | c\_u) p\_{\text{gen}}(y | c\_g = c(\tau))$$
where $\tau$ represents a trajectory of agentic actions. The pipeline operates through two primary modules:

Context-Aware Planning: This module functions at three hierarchical levels:

Context Grounding: This module gathers missing information from heterogeneous sources to resolve the context gap:

### Evaluation: IA-Bench [...] ### Findings

Experiments demonstrate that Qwen-Image-Agent significantly outperforms strong baselines and direct-generation models. Ablation studies confirm that the integrated agentic components are complementary, with significant performance drops observed when reasoning, search, or memory modules are disabled. The framework achieves state-of-the-art results on IA-Bench, WISE-Verified, and MindBench, validating that addressing the context gap is essential for practical, real-world image synthesis.
